results.title: ArchiMind pricing, case studies, alternatives & more
results.content:
Occupancy Load · Egress · Stairs & Ramps · Fire Resistance · Height & Area · Light & Ventilation · Chapter 11B Accessibility · Restroom Fixtures · Plumbing Fixture Count · EVCS Parking · Exit Signs · DSA Path of Travel

Every result is tied to a specific Revit element and cited to the exact code section. Not "something might be wrong." Exactly what, exactly where, exactly why.

Not Just Flagging — Fixing

Most compliance tools stop at flagging. ArchiMind goes further.

Click GoTo on any failing check — Revit zooms directly to the element. Click Fix — ArchiMind corrects it automatically. Door too narrow? Fixed. Handrail too low? Fixed. Ramp too steep? Fixed. Stair too narrow? Fixed. Corridor too tight? Fixed. Every fix is fully undoable with Ctrl+Z. Re-run the analysis to confirm. [...] Yes. The Enterprise license includes cloud dashboard sync so the whole team can track compliance status across projects.

ArchiMind currently supports Autodesk Revit 2023,2024, 2025 and 2026 on Windows.

Yes, accessible routes, parking, restroom accessibility, reach ranges, ramp slopes, and EVCS stalls are all checked under Chapter 11B.

Yes. ArchiMind includes a Live mode that monitors your Revit model continuously and flags compliance issues in real time as you design, so problems are caught during design development, not at permit submittal.

## Comments

Share your experience, review, or opinion about “ArchiMind”. [...] ArchiMind ends that.

ArchiMind is a native Revit plugin that turns California building code into a live, intelligent layer inside your model. The moment you run an analysis, every room, door, stair, corridor, and restroom is evaluated against CBC 2025 — automatically, instantly, and without leaving Revit. No exports. No spreadsheets. No guesswork.

Built for the Code That Actually Governs California Projects

California doesn't run on IBC.

ArchiMind was built from the ground up for this stack:

results.title: ArchiMind — CBC Compliance for Revit
results.content:
### Only re-checks what changed

Edits are tracked incrementally and dependencies followed — a wall change re-checks the rooms it affects, not the whole model. Your dashboard updates as you go.

Auto-fix

## Not just a list of errors — the correction, applied

Where the fix is unambiguous, ArchiMind makes the change in your model for you. Every fix runs in a named Revit transaction, so it's fully undoable with Ctrl+Z.

Every change is a single undo away — nothing is committed silently.

One-click fixes

Stair width to code minimum

Riser height & tread depth

Ramp slope corrected to 1:12

Ramp width to code minimum

Handrail height adjustment

Guard height adjustment

What gets checked

## Every finding cites the code section behind it [...] CBC ANALYSIS · RUNNINGsample-model · archimindcbc.com

ArchiMind checking walls, rooms, doors, and windows inside Revit — live recording.

Live mode

## Compliance checked as you design — not at plan check

Turn on Go Live and ArchiMind becomes a continuous code layer in your model. Catch issues the moment they appear, while they're still a two-second fix.

### Watches every edit

Move a door, add a room, change a wall — ArchiMind picks up the change automatically. No re-run button, no export step.

### Flags right on the element

Failing elements turn red and warnings amber directly in your active view, each tied to the CBC section it violates. Pass, and the flag clears itself.

### Only re-checks what changed [...] ## Every finding cites the code section behind it

ArchiMind checks across more than 40 CBC sections, for both the 2025 and 2022 editions. When something fails, you get the exact section to defend it — not a black-box flag.

Chapter 10

### Egress & exits

§1006, §1010, §1011, §1013–1017, §1020, §1031

Chapter 11B

### Accessibility & DSA

Path of travel, DSA IR 10.2 & 11B-10

Chapters 6 & 7

### Fire resistance

§601, §602, §508.4 separations

Chapter 5

### Height & area

§504, §506, §508 allowable area

Chapter 12

### Light & ventilation

§1203, §1205, §1207, §1208

Chapter 29

### Plumbing fixtures

Required fixture counts by occupancy

Chapter 10

### Occupant load

§1004 load factors & classification

CALGreen

### EV charging

EVCS space provisioning

How it works

results.title: Archimind - AI Tutor for Nepal Students
results.content:
AI-powered learning for Nepal

# Your Personal AI Tutor forSEE & NEB Exams

Archimind helps students across Nepal learn smarter with AI-powered tutoring, curated teacher notes, and practice questions aligned to the national curriculum.

Start Learning NowPractice Questions

### Chat with AI

Ask any question about your subjects and get instant, clear explanations tailored to the Nepal curriculum.

Start Chatting### Knowledge Exchange

A democratic peer-to-peer library. Share notes, links, and study materials -- upvote what helps most.

Browse & Share### SEE/NEB Practice

Practice with past exam questions and model questions to boost your exam confidence.

Start Practice### Discovery Hub [...] Start Practice### Discovery Hub

Real-time science news from NASA, Nature, and more. Stay curious -- great scientists are great readers first.

## Unreliable for Precise Interior Design Work
I began using Archsynth for my interior design business and was ultimately very disappointed. While the platform is capable of rendering attractive random images and switching between different visual styles, it fails completely when asked to produce anything precise or directive.
